data.title: AI memory, chip stocks slump as sell-off continues amid global peers tumbling
data.description:
Shares of memory and AI-related stocks tumbled even further on Thursday after opening in the green following yesterday's sell-off.

Wall Street’s major indexes were mixed on Thursday due to a weaker-than-expected June non-farm payrolls report that lowered expectations for the Federal Reserve to hike interest rates, and chip stocks dragging tech down. The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) fell about 1.35%. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) declined about 0.55%, but the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was the silver lining and rose about 0.42%. According to the latest survey conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors, or AAII, investor sentiment deteriorated sharply, with bearish views surging and optimism fading.

Wednesday's tech sell-off had pressured Asian peers, which opened in the red and tumbled during the day. South Korea’s Kospi Index slumped about 7.89%. South Korean memory chip giant SK Hynix (HXSCL) plummeted nearly 15%, while peer Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) tumbled around 9% on the Korea Exchange. Earlier this week, the two countries pledged billions in investment in South Korea aimed at growing the country's semiconductors and AI space. Today the two provided details. 

SK Hynix said it would invest 100T won (about $64.38B) to build memory chip ‌and packaging plants as part of a South Korean investment drive, which was announced earlier this week, aimed at semiconductors and AI. Meanwhile, Samsung plans to invest a combined 140T won ($90B) in South Korea's central Chungcheong region, expanding production of display panels, batteries, semiconductors, and chip materials. Reportedly, SK Hynix is removing price caps from its Long-Term Agreements, or LTAs, recently signed with its clients. Analysts suggest that suppliers' negotiating power is being significantly strengthened as the company, along with rival Samsung, extends contract periods.

"Samsung and SK Hynix now make up around half the Kospi’s total weight, up from around just a quarter at the end of last year," Zavier Wong, analyst at eToro, said in a note, according to CNBC. This highlights the companies' huge influence on the country’s benchmark index.

Hong Kong and Chinese tech stocks also saw red. China’s largest contract chipmaker and foundry Semiconductor Manufacturing International tumbled about 10% in Hong Kong, while Hua Hong Grace, another Chinese chip foundry listed in Hong Kong, slumped nearly 13%.

American memory giant Micron Technology (MU) — which competes with Samsung and SK Hynix to provide High-Bandwidth Memory, or HBM chips to Nvidia — fell nearly 5% on Thursday. Sandisk (SNDK) plummeted nearly 13%, while Western Digital (WDC) slumped about 10%. During the second quarter of 2026, Night Watch Investment Management LP had trimmed its position in Western Digital. Seagate Technology (STX) also slumped about 10%.

Shares of AI chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) dipped around 2%, while Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) slumped nearly 5%. Qualcomm (QCOM) also fell about 5%, while Broadcom (AVGO) declined around 3%. Cerebras (CBRS) slumped about 7%.

Other AI and networking-related stocks also tumbled on Thursday. Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) plummeted about 17%, while Corning (GLW) slumped nearly 11%. Coherent (COHR) tumbled around 10%, while Lumentum (LITE) fell nearly 9%. Ciena (CIEN) and Celestica (CLS) each declined about 8%. Arista Networks (ANET) fell nearly 5%, while Cisco (CSCO) declined about 4%.

Shares of Marvell Technology (MRVL), GlobalFoundries (GFS), and Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) each tumbled nearly 10%. Arm (ARM) slumped about 7%, while Intel (INTC) fell nearly 5% despite positive news. HSBC raised Intel's price target to a street-high of $200 from $100, while keeping its Buy rating. Meanwhile, Wedbush noted that Tesla's hiring of Gary Jiang, an 18-year veteran of Intel, to help with its Terafab project is considered a potential “positive” for the chip giant.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and Analog Devices (ADI) each fell about 3%, while Texas Instruments (TXN) dipped nearly 2%.

Aehr Test Systems (AEHR) slumped about 20%. Aehr was removed from a broad range of Russell indexes — including the Russell 3000E and Russell Microcap as well as the Russell 2000 Value —during the annual index reconstitution, which went into effect on June 27.

Chip equipment makers: KLA (KLAC) slumped about 13%, Lam Research (LRCX) tumbled nearly 11%, and Applied Materials (AMAT) each declined about 10%. ASML (ASML) fell nearly 5% on Thursday.

Update: The story was updated with stock price movement.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: RKLB, XOM, QCOM, DELL
data.description:
Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Rocket Lab (RKLB) have received upgrades based on favorable industry dynamics and strategic acquisitions, respectively. Meanwhile, Dell Technologies (DELL) and Qualcomm (QCOM) face downgrades as analysts point to stretched valuations and elevated execution risks following significant rallies.

Upgrades

  • Exxon Mobil (XOM): Upgrade to Buy by Envision Research. The analyst cites multi-decade lows in both U.S. and global oil inventories, along with a depleted Strategic Petroleum Reserve, as catalysts for a near-term price rebound combined with recent valuation compression.

    "With both US and global inventories at such low levels, I see large odds for a restocking in the near term (say in the next 1–2 years). As an example, according to the OECD model quoted above, the refiling of the global inventory is projected to begin in early 2027. I further expect the refilling of both US and global reserves to act as a catalyst for oil price recovery."

  • Rocket Lab (RKLB): Upgrade to Speculative Buy by JR Research. The analyst views the Iridium acquisition as a pivotal move that provides critical L-band spectrum access and proven EBITDA profitability, positioning the company to compete more effectively against integrated players.

    "For RKLB, I think we need to see management providing more hard numbers in terms of clarity on how this Iridium M&A can really reshape its growth trajectory. That's kind of an ambivalent moment right now, which could either lift its buying sentiments or see the selling pressure intensify."

Downgrades

  • Dell Technologies (DELL): Downgrade to Hold by The Alpha Analyst. After delivering approximately 360% returns, the analyst believes the stock now trades at fair-to-expensive valuations with diminished asymmetry, recommending profit taking.

    "The asymmetry that lower valuations and a prospective earnings revision spree ahead provided even in January this year is no longer available. The demand strength and overall fundamentals do show sufficient strength to keep the stock supported—but the upside route from here is not more obvious than downside risks today."

  • Qualcomm (QCOM): Downgrade to Hold by Kumquat Research. The analyst notes that Qualcomm's pivot from smartphones to AI chips introduces heightened volatility, margin pressure, and significant execution risk over a lengthy transition period.

    "Qualcomm is now leaving the comfort of a domain that it has dominated for decades and is entering uncharted territory; with that move comes execution risk and margin risks, for which current shareholders might not have the risk tolerance."

data.title: Cumberland Farms files for IPO
data.description:
Massachusetts-based convenience store chain Cumberland Farms filed a registration statement with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission ahead of a potential initial public offering.

The number of shares and price range have yet to be determined.

The shares will trade on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol CMBY.

The company began as a one-cow dairy farm in Cumberland, Rhode Island, in 1939, with the first dairy store opening in Bellingham, Massachusetts, 20 years later. The chain eventually spread across New England and Florida, largely by acquiring Gulf and Chevron gas stations in 10 Northeastern states in the mid-1980s and then 200 Exxon stations in 2003.

With 1,500 retail locations, Cumberland Farms is one of the fastest-growing convenience store chains in the U.S.   

In addition to Cumberland Farms, the parent company’s portfolio includes Certified Oil, Fastrac, Kwik Shop, Loaf’N Jug, Minit Mart, Sprint Food Stores, Tom Thumb, Turkey Hill, and Quik Stop stores.

The IPO relates solely to Cumberland Farms.

data.title: Boeing making progress in reducing Air Force One technical challenges, GAO says
data.description:
Boeing (BA) has reduced some of the technical challenges that have delayed its delivery of two jets that will serve as Air Force One, the Government Accountability Office said in its annual assessment of U.S. weapons programs, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

The company likely will deliver the first of the modified jets by 2028 and has made progress on "completing the engineering design for the environmental control system, resolving cabin decompression issues, and hiring and retaining more qualified mechanics," the GAO said.

The Air Force One modifications, which started in 2020, still need "detailed designs for the aircraft interiors, fabrication of the wire bundles, and rework to correct defects in structural modifications," and repairs of stress corrosion cracks on the two planes "are ongoing and are expected to be completed" this year, officials told GAO.

Boeing (BA) remains nearly three years behind its original schedule to deliver the jets, GAO said, with each estimated to cost $3.3B, or ~7% more than last year's projection.

Separately, UPS (UPS) said it relied on Boeing's (BA) assessment that a known engine mount issue did not pose a flight safety risk when it chose not to adopt enhanced inspections before last year's fatal cargo plane crash in Louisville, according to newly released National Transportation Safety Board filings.

In its post-hearing submission to the NTSB, UPS (UPS) said it followed all required Boeing (BA) and Federal Aviation Administration-approved maintenance programs for its MD-11 fleet.

data.title: Meta rolls out new gaming app, Pocket
data.description:
• Meta Platforms (META) is rolling out a new gaming app called “Pocket” that lets users create “gizmos” or mini-games by typing out an AI prompt.
  • Meta describes Pocket as an app “where you can create, share, and discover gizmos with friends. A gizmo is an interactive, playable AI-generated experience.”
  • Last March, Meta (META) acquired a non-exclusive license to Atma Sciences technology, the company behind an app called “Gizmo,” and hired the team credited with its development.
  • “You can create your own gizmo just by typing what you want to create,” Meta says on its website.
  • However, Meta (META) also notes that disclosing interactions with gizmos on Pocket will be used to improve AI at Meta.
  • Pocket is currently only available in certain regions.

data.title: Waystar rises as KeyBanc initiates at Overweight on valuation disconnect
data.description:
Shares of Waystar Holding (WAY) spiked on Thursday after KeyBanc Capital Markets initiated coverage of the health tech with an Overweight rating and a $30 price target, citing a valuation disconnect between the company and its strategic advantages.

Analyst Scott Schoenhaus argued that Waystar (WAY), which provides a cloud-based software platform for healthcare payments, is trading at a level that doesn’t reflect its growth profile and strong margins.

Based on a survey of hospital executives, Schoenhaus argued that 25% of respondents now use Waystar after switching from UnitedHealth’s (UNH) Change Healthcare, and 75% of them are very satisfied with their new service provider.

Additionally, citing KeyBanc’s proprietary credit card data, the analyst noticed an uptick in healthcare utilization and, therefore, in WAY’s volume-based revenue in June. "From our proprietary data, while we saw some moderation in 1Q and in May, preliminary data for healthcare spend in June looks improved," Schoenhaus wrote.

He ruled out a potential impact from AI on Waystar (WAY), arguing that it will be difficult for large language models to replicate or replace the company’s end-to-end platform, which brings together 5,000 payers and over 1M providers.

data.title: UBS rebukes Swiss central bank's report about adequate capital for proposed rules - report
data.description:
UBS Group (UBS) already has sufficient capital to meet the proposed new rules, according to the Swiss central bank.

A proposed legislation in Switzerland requires UBS to fully back its foreign units with ​common equity Tier 1 capital. The new rule is intended to toughen legislation to prevent a repeat of the Credit Suisse meltdown.

The new rule was anticipated to require UBS to strengthen its CET1 capital by ~$20B. The Swiss bank was opposing the proposal, saying that they would hurt its ability to compete globally.

Notably, the Financial Stability Report 2026 released by the Swiss National Bank today said, "UBS exceeds its fully applied capital requirements under the current 'too big to fail' ((TBTF)) regulations applicable as of 2030."

"Regarding liquidity, experience from 2022 – 2023 in Switzerland and the U.S. shows that outflows can deplete liquidity buffers rapidly, even if these buffers are substantial. Some banks may also face the risk of liquidity shortfalls in foreign currencies. It is therefore essential that banks prepare sufficient collateral eligible for accessing liquidity support from the SNB (Extended Liquidity Facility, ELF) and, where applicable, from foreign central banks," said the report.

"The proposal of the Federal Council addresses these risks in a targeted manner and primarily affects UBS," said the report.

"According to the pro forma calculations of the authorities and including reserves, UBS already has sufficient capital to meet the proposed requirements," said the report.

However, UBS has criticized the central bank for the "misleading" report, according to a Bloomberg article published separately.

"Today's report by the SNB continues to reiterate misleading statements, including an incomplete analysis of the root causes of the collapse of Credit Suisse, the role of AT1, and the impact of Basel 3 capital rules, rather than offering the distinct independent analysis needed for a fact-based policy debate that is critical to the future resilience of Switzerland's financial center," said the Swiss lender in an emailed statement to Bloomberg.

"We note that the sizable and unwarranted cumulative capital impact of the proposed measures would be a significant competitive disadvantage both domestically and internationally – as recognized by truly independent observers, including a leading credit rating agency," said the statement.

data.title: Lindsay outlines Brazil FINAME rate cut to 11.5% while expecting restructuring savings to begin in fiscal 2027
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Lindsay Corporation (LNN) Q3 2026

Management View

  • "Trade uncertainty, high input costs and weak farmer sentiment continue to weigh on our business." (President, CEO & Director Randy Wood)
  • "In North America, our irrigation customers continue to delay large capital purchases given current farm economics, which resulted in lower unit sales volumes in the quarter." (President, CEO & Director Wood)
  • "In our international business, revenues were down slightly year-over-year, driven by lower sales volumes in Brazil due to the high interest rate environment and limited access to credit." (President, CEO & Director Wood)
  • "Our infrastructure business continued to grow year-over-year. Third quarter revenues increased 8%, driven by higher road safety product revenues, marking 3 consecutive quarters of growth." (President, CEO & Director Wood)
  • "We expect these dynamics to support sustained double-digit technology revenue growth in fiscal 2026." (President, CEO & Director Wood)
  • "We have taken the initiative to restructure and rightsize portions of our organization and optimize our operating cost structure... We expect savings to begin in fiscal 2027." (President, CEO & Director Wood)
  • "Total revenues for the third quarter of fiscal 2026 were $160.8 million, a decrease of 5% compared to $169.5 million in the prior year." (CFO & Senior VP Samuel Hinrichsen)
  • "Third quarter results include a onetime benefit related to tariff refunds." (CFO & Senior VP Hinrichsen)

Outlook

  • "We do not expect a meaningful near-term recovery in North American demand until these economics improve." (President, CEO & Director Wood)
  • "We are also pleased to see the FINAME financing rate under Brazil's 2026, 2027 crop plan decline from 12.5% to 11.5%." (President, CEO & Director Wood)
  • "At the same time... the total funding allocated to irrigation within the FINAME program has been reduced from approximately BRL 2.75 billion to BRL 1.7 billion." (President, CEO & Director Wood)
  • "We will continue delivery of the large irrigation project through our fiscal fourth quarter." (President, CEO & Director Wood)
  • "Our new galvanizing facility remains on schedule, and we expect that to be turned over to production in early 2027." (President, CEO & Director Wood)

Financial Results

  • "Operating income for the third quarter was $18.5 million compared to $23.8 million in the prior year, and operating margin was 11.5% of sales compared to 14% of sales last year." (CFO & Senior VP Hinrichsen)
  • "Net earnings for the quarter were $15.8 million or $1.53 per diluted share compared to $19.5 million or $1.78 per diluted share in the prior year." (CFO & Senior VP Hinrichsen)
  • "Irrigation segment revenues for the third quarter were $133 million, a decrease of 7% compared to $143.7 million in the prior year." (CFO & Senior VP Hinrichsen)
  • "In our Infrastructure segment, revenues for the quarter increased 8% to $27.7 million compared to $25.7 million in the prior year." (CFO & Senior VP Hinrichsen)
  • "At the end of the third quarter, our total available liquidity was $204.8 million, which includes $154.8 million in cash and cash equivalents and $50 million available under our revolving credit facility." (CFO & Senior VP Hinrichsen)
  • "During the first 9 months of the fiscal year, we have returned $80.7 million to shareholders through share repurchases." (CFO & Senior VP Hinrichsen)

Q&A

  • Ryan Connors, Northcoast Research Partners, LLC: "could you kind of expand on the outlook [in the Middle East]"; President, CEO & Director Wood: "we haven't seen any significant shift in the total market opportunity... there's still, in our view, a lot more business out there. We're in the early innings."
  • Ryan Connors, Northcoast Research Partners, LLC: "the $80 million... $70 million in the current fiscal year"; CFO Hinrichsen: "$70 million is still the expectation to be recognized in fiscal '26. The remaining $10 million will spill into 2027."
  • Ryan Connors, Northcoast Research Partners, LLC: "were you referring to fiscal '27 or calendar '27"; President, CEO & Director Wood: "That will be calendar '27."
  • Ryan Connors, Northcoast Research Partners, LLC: CapEx after galvanizing; CFO Hinrichsen: "Once the galvanizing goes live, you will see a return to normalized capital levels."
  • Ryan Connors, Northcoast Research Partners, LLC: AI for irrigation; President, CEO & Director Wood: "with FieldNet Advisor specifically, we've really been deploying a lot of AI models to help with irrigation scheduling" and "machine learning can allow us to pre-diagnose failures" in SmartPivot.
  • Brian Drab, William Blair: Brazil growth wording; President, CEO & Director Wood: "We wouldn't expect a significant impact on our fourth quarter" and "we could see some of those early in first quarter fiscal year '27."
  • Brian Drab, William Blair: Q4 seasonality; President, CEO & Director Wood: "the fourth quarter historically... is the lowest volume quarter for us" and "we aren't currently projecting a lot of optimism relative to volume in the fourth quarter."
  • Brian Drab, William Blair: gross margin color; CFO Hinrichsen: "you will see the impact of more unfavorable absorption" and "there's too many variables to be very discrete on the expectations."
  • Adam Farley, Stifel: drought conditions; President, CEO & Director Wood: "a year ago... about 15% of the country" in severe-to-exceptional drought and "this year, it's over 1/3 of the country" plus "drought is generally good for business until it isn't."
  • Adam Farley, Stifel: restructuring details; President, CEO & Director Wood: "we'll probably wait until we get through the full quarter before we get a little more specific" and "this is about organization structure, efficiency... leveraging tools like AI to change how we work."
  • Jon Braatz, Kansas City Capital Associates: domestic pricing; President, CEO & Director Wood: "it does get a lot more competitive" and "We're never going to use pricing to drive volume... But certainly... a more competitive environment."
  • Jon Braatz, Kansas City Capital Associates: corporate expense savings; CFO Hinrichsen: "managing everything from discretionary spending to timing of expenses" and "restructuring actions... will yield savings in our fiscal 2027."
  • Brett Kearney: new road safety products traction; President, CEO & Director Wood: "I can't comment on kind of early penetration and sales volume" and "once we get through final testing and release, we do have... pretty good outlook... continuing the growth trend."

Sentiment Analysis

  • "I wanted to start on the Middle East project... any update on kind of the pipeline" and "it sounded a little bit more cautious" captured analyst tone focused on project timing, Brazil visibility, margins, and restructuring specificity. (Ryan Connors, Northcoast Research Partners, LLC; Brian Drab, William Blair; Adam Farley, Stifel)
  • "we remain cautiously optimistic in the short term while maintaining a high degree of confidence in the long-term growth opportunity in Brazil" and "we remain confident in our strategy" reflected management’s prepared-remarks tone, while Q&A responses included qualifiers such as "it's probably too early" and "we'll probably wait" on restructuring specifics. (President, CEO & Director Randy Wood)
  • "we do not expect any meaningful market recovery until the new crop plan is released in July" in the prior quarter shifted to "we could see some of those early in first quarter fiscal year '27" after the crop plan details were released, with management adding a more explicit constraint discussion around program funding. (President, CEO & Director Wood)

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • "we don't anticipate a large Road Zipper project this fiscal year" remained consistent with the prior quarter’s "we did not expect to repeat" a prior-year Road Zipper delivery, while Q3 emphasized "3 consecutive quarters of growth" in road safety products. (President, CEO & Director Randy Wood)
  • "the FINAME financing rate... decline from 12.5% to 11.5%" replaced prior-quarter conditional language that rates were "expected" to be lower, while Q3 also added that irrigation funding was "reduced... to BRL 1.7 billion" as a near-term limiter. (President, CEO & Director Wood)
  • "Our new tube mill has been successfully commissioned and is now in full production" advanced the prior-quarter status that the tube mill was "turned over to full production," while the galvanizing timeline shifted from "come online in early 2027" to clarification that turnover is "calendar '27" in Q&A. (President, CEO & Director Wood)

Risks and Concerns

  • "Trade uncertainty, high input costs and weak farmer sentiment continue to weigh on our business" and "input cost escalate during the fiscal year and our pricing actions still need to catch up" framed near-term pressure, with management pointing to "pricing, cost management and operational efficiency" as key levers. (President, CEO & Director Randy Wood; CFO & Senior VP Samuel Hinrichsen)
  • "the availability of credit remains a constraint" in Brazil was paired with the caution that reduced FINAME funding "could limit the pace of market expansion in the near term," despite the rate cut. (President, CEO & Director Wood)
  • "it's the one where we have the lowest amount of fixed overhead absorption" described Q4 seasonality risk, while management also flagged a "more competitive" domestic pricing environment that "can obviously create a little bit of pinch on margins." (President, CEO & Director Wood)

Final Takeaway

Management described a cyclical downturn in irrigation demand tied to farm economics, trade uncertainty, and constrained credit—particularly in Brazil—while highlighting an 8% year-over-year revenue increase in infrastructure and continued road safety growth. Leadership pointed to connected-equipment initiatives, including AI-enabled FieldNet Advisor and SmartPivot diagnostics, and stated it expects sustained double-digit technology revenue growth in fiscal 2026. The call also introduced a restructuring and rightsizing initiative with savings expected to begin in fiscal 2027, alongside continued delivery of a large MENA project into fiscal Q4 and ongoing manufacturing investments, including a tube mill now in full production and a galvanizing facility planned for calendar 2027.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Bullish sentiment falls sharply while bearish views rise, AAII survey shows
data.description:
Investor sentiment deteriorated sharply, with bearish views surging and optimism fading, according to the latest survey conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors (AAII).

The American Association of Individual Investors said in its survey that the bullish camp plunged to 31.4% for the week ended July 1, from 44.9% in the prior week. Meanwhile, bearish sentiment rose to 42.3%, compared to 36.1% last week.

Ongoing market volatility in the global markets due to the turmoil between Iran and the U.S. as well as the intense selling pressure in the tech sector has kept investors on their toes.

According to AAII, neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, rose to 26.4%, compared to 18.9% last week.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: 7-Eleven sues Nike over Air Max 95 stripe design
data.description:
7-Eleven has sued Nike (NKE), alleging the sportswear giant's upcoming Air Max 95 sneaker infringes its trademark by using a stripe design resembling the convenience store chain's signature orange, green, and red branding.

The lawsuit, filed on Wednesday in federal court in Texas, says the shoe is set for release on July 11, a date associated with 7-Eleven Day and the retailer's annual Free Slurpee Day promotion. It alleges the design could lead consumers to mistakenly believe the shoe is sponsored or endorsed by 7-Eleven.

The retailer said it has used the tricolor design for decades and owns multiple trademark registrations covering it. It also alleged it unsuccessfully sought to resolve the dispute before filing suit.

7-Eleven is seeking to block sales of the shoe, recall products already distributed, and recover monetary damages and Nike's profits from the footwear.
